title: BCI Competition 2020 Track 5 — ERP during walking (scalp, ear-EEG, and IMU)
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 15 healthy participants performing a visual P300 oddball task while walking on a treadmill at 1.6 m/s. The study includes simultaneous acquisition from scalp EEG (46 channels), ear-EEG (6 channels), EOG (4 channels), and forehead-mounted IMU sensors, sampled at 100 Hz. The dataset was collected for BCI Competition 2020 Track 5 to evaluate brain-computer interface performance in ambulatory conditions, with 300 trials per subject (180 training, 60 validation, 60 test) and preprocessed data epoched from -190 ms to +800 ms around visual stimulus presentation.
methods_description: Data were acquired at 100 Hz sampling rate from 56 channels including scalp EEG (46 channels, standard 10-05 montage), ear-EEG (6 channels), EOG (4 channels), and IMU sensors (3-axis accelerometer and gyroscope). Participants performed a visual oddball P300 task with target ('OOO') and non-target ('XXX') stimuli (target probability 0.2) while walking on a treadmill at 1.6 m/s. Trials were epoched from -190 ms to +800 ms around stimulus onset. Data were preprocessed and sampled at 100 Hz with 50 Hz line frequency filtering.
